description: Two dedup queues are defined (non-partitioned, no concurrency limit): 'context.consolidate.dedup' and 'context.compile.dedup'
example: new WorkflowQueue('context.consolidate.dedup') — no concurrency or partitionQueue options
name: dedup_queues_defined

description: The original work queues ('context.consolidate', 'context.compile') retain their partitionQueue:true and concurrency settings
name: work_queues_unchanged

description: Wrapper workflows are registered with DBOS for both consolidate and compile, with names 'context.consolidate.dedup' and 'context.compile.dedup'
name: wrapper_workflows_registered

description: A DBOS step is registered to fetch the latest entry's updatedAt for dedup key computation — not inline code
name: latest_entry_step_registered

description: The deduplicationID is a hash of diaryId + params + latestEntry.updatedAt (not just diaryId + params)
name: dedup_key_includes_updated_at

description: The HTTP route enqueues on the dedup queue (not the work queue directly), passing deduplicationID
name: http_route_enqueues_on_dedup_queue

description: DBOSQueueDuplicatedError is caught and translated to HTTP 409 (not 500 or ignored)
name: duplicate_returns_409

description: The work queue enqueue (inside the wrapper workflow) does NOT pass deduplicationID — only queuePartitionKey
name: no_dedup_on_work_queue

description: A test verifies that a duplicate request (same params, same diary state) returns HTTP 409
name: test_duplicate_returns_409
